is a Pakistani actress who predominantly appears in Pakistani television serials. She earlier starred as recurring roles and mostly plays the role of mothers. She made her cinematic debut in [Azfar Jafri](/wiki/Azfar_Jafri "Azfar Jafri")'s film *[Heer Maan Ja](/wiki/Heer_Maan_Ja "Heer Maan Ja")* and later starred in *[Parde Mein Rehne Do](/wiki/Parde_Mein_Rehne_Do "Parde Mein Rehne Do")*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Some of her notable television appearances in supporting roles include *Uraan*, *[Inkaar](/wiki/Inkaar_(TV_series) "Inkaar (TV series)")*, *[Kashf](/wiki/Kashf_(TV_series) "Kashf (TV series)")* and *[Khuda Aur Muhabbat](/wiki/Khuda_Aur_Muhabbat_(season_3) "Khuda Aur Muhabbat (season 3)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
["'Sometimes we take the nicest people for granted': Hania Aamir reflects on valuing people in life"](https://images.dawn.com/news/1188594). *DAWN Images*. 15 October 2021.

["Tackling taboos: 'Parde Mein Rehne Do' trailer promises vibrant dramedy on struggles of infertility"](https://tribune.com.pk/story/2345348/tackling-taboos-parde-mein-rehne-do-trailer-promises-vibrant-dramedy-on-struggles-of-infertility?). *Express Tribune*. 26 February 2022. Retrieved 20 March 2022.

["Imran Ashraf and Yumna Zaidi come together for Inkaar"](https://www.thenews.com.pk/magazine/instep-today/434914-imran-ashraf-and-yumna-zaidi-come-together-for-inkaar). *[The News International](/wiki/The_News_International "The News International")*. 21 February 2019.
["Review: Kashf concludes on a high after a riveting run"](https://cutacut.com/2020/10/28/review-kashf-concludes-on-a-high-after-a-riveting-run/). *Cutacut*. 28 October 2020.

["Nimra Khan And Munazzah Arif Open Up About Non-Payment By Producer"](https://propakistani.pk/lens/nimra-khan-and-munazzah-arif-open-up-about-non-payment-by-producer/). *propakistani.pk*. 4 November 2021.

["Teasers are out for Ahad Raza Mir and Ramsha Khan's Ramazan special Hum Tum"](https://images.dawn.com/news/1189762/teasers-are-out-for-ahad-raza-mir-and-ramsha-khans-ramazan-special-hum-tum). *Dawn Images*. 22 March 2022.

Yousaf, Gulbahar (13 July 2019). ["'Ranjha Ranjha Kardi' unravels the harsh realities of life"](https://dailytimes.com.pk/428980/ranjha-ranjha-kardi-unravels-the-harsh-realities-of-life/). *[Daily Times](/wiki/Daily_Times_(Pakistan) "Daily Times (Pakistan)")*.

["Inkaar picks up pace in recent episode"](https://www.thenews.com.pk/magazine/instep-today/506256-inkaar-picks-up-pace-in-recent-episode). *[The News International](/wiki/The_News_International "The News International")*. 31 July 2019.
["Qurbatain HUM TV's newest presentation that revolves around friendship, lust, and deceit"](https://www.oyeyeah.com/entertainment/qurbatain-hum-tvs-newest-presentation/). 6 July 2020.
["Ayeza Khan signed up for another bubby character in Laapata'"](https://www.incpak.com/entertainment/ayeza-khan-laa-pata). *INCPAK*. 27 May 2022.

["Trailblazing Ramadan specials 'Hum Tum' and 'Paristan' all set to air"](https://dailytimes.com.pk/912568/trailblazing-ramadan-specials-hum-tum-and-paristan-all-set-to-air/). *Daily Times*. 3 April 2022.
["Comedian Shafaat Ali is getting serious for his new role in Nayhar Ka Mazhar"](https://images.dawn.com/news/1188779). *Dawn Images*. 23 February 2022.

["Two new drama serials, 'Khush Naseebi' and 'Rang De', to premiere today on Geo"](https://www.thenews.pk/print/1405962-two-new-drama-serials-khush-naseebi-and-rang-de-to-premiere-today-on-geo). *The News International*. 24 March 2026.

first_paragraph: **Munazzah Arif** is a Pakistani actress who predominantly appears in Pakistani television serials. She earlier starred as recurring roles and mostly plays the role of mothers. She made her cinematic debut in [Azfar Jafri](/wiki/Azfar_Jafri "Azfar Jafri")'s film *[Heer Maan Ja](/wiki/Heer_Maan_Ja "Heer Maan Ja")* and later starred in *[Parde Mein Rehne Do](/wiki/Parde_Mein_Rehne_Do "Parde Mein Rehne Do")*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Some of her notable television appearances in supporting roles include *Uraan*, *[Inkaar](/wiki/Inkaar_(TV_series) "Inkaar (TV series)")*, *[Kashf](/wiki/Kashf_(TV_series) "Kashf (TV series)")* and *[Khuda Aur Muhabbat](/wiki/Khuda_Aur_Muhabbat_(season_3) "Khuda Aur Muhabbat (season 3)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani actress

| Munazzah Arif | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1968-03-18) 18 March 1968 (age 58)  [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Education | Lahore College for Women University |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 2007 – present |
| Children | 3 |

**Munazzah Arif** is a Pakistani actress who predominantly appears in Pakistani television serials. She earlier starred as recurring roles and mostly plays the role of mothers. She made her cinematic debut in [Azfar Jafri](/wiki/Azfar_Jafri "Azfar Jafri")'s film *[Heer Maan Ja](/wiki/Heer_Maan_Ja "Heer Maan Ja")* and later starred in *[Parde Mein Rehne Do](/wiki/Parde_Mein_Rehne_Do "Parde Mein Rehne Do")*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Some of her notable television appearances in supporting roles include *Uraan*, *[Inkaar](/wiki/Inkaar_(TV_series) "Inkaar (TV series)")*, *[Kashf](/wiki/Kashf_(TV_series) "Kashf (TV series)")* and *[Khuda Aur Muhabbat](/wiki/Khuda_Aur_Muhabbat_(season_3) "Khuda Aur Muhabbat (season 3)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
